items.title: 21yo man stabbed to death in crowd in Tennant Creek street

children.content: Police have arrested a 17-year-old boy after a man was fatally stabbed in a street in Tennant Creek overnight.

image.altText: Tennant creek from the top of a hill.

items.title: Creditors of NT mine with $360m debt criticise lack of oversight

children.content: In the wake of Nathan River Resources entering into voluntary administration, creditors have criticised the NT government for its lack of oversight of the struggling company. ASIC documents show the mining company owes more than $360 million.

image.altText: A man standing with his arms folded, in front of several long metal pipes in an outdoor work yard.

items.title: NT government axes controversial $1.1 million private midwife contract

children.content: The Northern Territory government has axed a $1.1 million contract awarded to a Darwin midwife, banned from working unsupervised, almost 10 months after doctors began raising concerns about the deal.

gallery.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

avatar.alt: A journalist wears a pattern blouse and serious facial expression.

items.title: Alleged drug dealer caught after trading cannabis for cat food

children.content: Police charge a 45-year-old woman with a range of drug, weapons and other offences after a group of teenagers were caught stealing cat food to allegedly swap with her for cannabis.

image.altText: A large collection of bags full of cannabis, laid out on a carpet.

items.title: NT government says Claravale land-clearing dispute is now settled

children.content: For years, the Northern Territory government has been locked in a legal battle with Claravale Station over allegations its owners cleared hundreds of hectares without a permit. But it is not the first time the government has announced the settlement deal.

image.altText: Cleared land with two lone trees in the middle.

items.title: Police dog puts alleged fugitive bikie in hospital

children.content: A Top End bikie who allegedly escaped police custody last week has been hospitalised after being bitten by a police dog during his re-capture.

image.altText: A dog stands on the back seat of a ute.

items.title: NT government questioned over youth prison lockdowns, police policy

children.content: As the NT's CLP government remains firm on its tough-on-crime agenda and rebuilding of the economy, the first week of budget estimates saw the government questioned around prison lockdowns, police policy and housing.

image.altText: Two curved tables facing each other, people sitting either side, most in formal office wear.

items.title: The sword that links feared pirates to Britain's greatest naval hero

children.content: The history of a wavy-bladed sword, bought at a Brisbane gun show around the end of the 1980s, begins with Moro raiders resisting Spanish colonists in the Philippines, but may also include Admiral Lord Nelson.

image.altText: A sword with a broad, wavy blade and hemp-wrapped and carved wooden handle, lying next to a wooden sheath.

items.title: Britain's defence strategy under fire while global nuclear spending surges

children.content: The prospect of nuclear weapons being used in either Ukraine or the Middle East, rather than being fired between Moscow, Washington or Beijing has risen.

image.altText: John Healey providing a press conference indoors, wearing a suit, tie and glasses.

US players celebrate the opening goal against Paraguay together

articles.title: 'A footballer's dream': From humble beginnings to the world stage

children.content: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

image.altText: composite of a Mo Toure as a young boy in a soccer field and him as an adult in a Socceroos guernsey

articles.title: Monterrey Stadium one of the most stunning World Cup venues in history

children.content: From volcano mimics to modern super-stadia with retractable roofs, the grounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup are among the most spectacular on Earth. Here's your guide to each of the 16 stadiums across Canada, Mexico and the United States.

image.altText: General view of Monterrey Stadium

articles.title: How far can the Socceroos really go at the FIFA World Cup?

children.content: This crop of Socceroos has been labelled the "next generation", but that doesn't mean they aren't aiming high at the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Australia players pose for fans during the FIFA Series 2026

children.content: World Cup co-host USA gets its campaign underway against Paraguay after Canada rescued a late draw with Bosnia and Herzegovina in Toronto. Follow live.

updates.title: HT: What a half from Team USA

items.title: How Israel's operation 'Eternal Darkness' in southern Lebanon is expanding

children.content: The so-called ceasefire since April, which Hezbollah is not a party to, has done little to halt the death and destruction in southern Lebanon. Here's how the scale of the Israeli operation called "Eternal Darkness" continues to expand.

image.altText: A green landscape dotted with houses in southern Lebanon is seen from Metula.

items.title: 'Seeking connection': The unseen children who roam the streets at night

children.content: Youth Beat workers take to Perth streets at night to help young people who have nowhere else to go.

image.altText: Two teenage girls walk between large brick pylons in Perth's CBD at night.

items.title: Grandfather of child rescued from Syria pleads for 'stability, love, care'

children.content: Jasmine was five years old and so traumatised that when she woke up trying to scream, she couldn't make a sound.

items.title: A man fled a 'bounty' in Canberra. Months later, he was dead in Brisbane

children.content: Neil Tracey's death was ruled an overdose in what investigators would call an open and shut case. His family are questioning if police knew about the bounty that led him to flee his hometown.

image.altText: Graphic of a man's smiling face with newspapers articles surrounding it.

items.title: Australia has valuable cards to play in the AI future, new report finds

children.content: A new report says Australia can capitalise on AI, with valuable cards to play strategically.

items.title: Reuben Kaye reckons you can joke about anything — 'it's just how you do it'

children.content: The new artistic director of the Adelaide Cabaret Festival shares what's unique about the art form in Australia and what his teenage self would think about who he is today.

gallery.altText: A portrait of Reuben Kaye, 41, smiling, mouth open, one finger between his teeth, with a cheeky expression.

items.title: New ambassador to US takes up tariffs, AUKUS causes

children.content: Greg Moriarty says he will "robustly" push back on the Trump administration's "extremely disappointing" new tariff plan and may recruit Australian businesses to help take up the fight.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Greg Moriarty smile and hold a document in the Oval Office.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Testing your knowledge of ABBA, the World Cup mascots and some fruity language

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: The highs and lows of having a pet

children.content: Pets can be lifelong companions, but they can also test us in ways we never imagined. We asked you to share your stories. Here's what you told us.

items.title: These ocean users have different ways of protecting themselves from sharks

children.content: After the deaths of three spearfishers from shark attacks, the conversation has turned to which shark deterrent measures are in place across the country and which ones have been found to work well.

image.altText: A man with short dark and grey hair, wearing a black t-shirt, standing in a workshop.

items.title: Villages 700 years old destroyed to 'uproot people' from their history

children.content: Non-governmental organisations Biladi and Blue Shield are among the design experts using new technologies to create detailed records of cultural heritage and help rebuild after conflict.

items.title: 'Special player and person': NZ cricket legend Williamson retires

children.content: New Zealand cricketing legend Kane Williamson announces his retirement from the game immediately after a stunning career.

items.title: As work modernises in Australia, some rare trades face decline

children.content: A shortage of locksmiths in Australia persists despite government-funded incentives. The nation's changing labour landscape has meant some specialist trades are not being chosen by apprentices.

image.altText: a man in a navy shirt outside a white trade van on a street

children.content: When Lillian Rowsell was in her final year of school and unable to continue formal education, her parents found a creative solution.

items.title: Ex-Liberal strategist, pollster Stansfield in Senate tilt

children.content: A former Liberal adviser turned Tasmanian political commentator says he is "genuinely worried about the future of the country" and plans to stand for preselection for the Senate at the next federal election.

image.altText: Brad Stansfield smiles at the camera.

items.title: Taylor Swift, Alanis Morissette inducted into Songwriters Hall of Fame

children.content: Taylor Swift has become the youngest woman to be inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame. She's among the writers behind some of pop's biggest hits to be inducted in 2026.

image.altText: Taylor Swift with hair tied back, red lipstick, and formal clothing in front of songwriters hall of fame signage

items.title: From Southbank to the Skyneedle: The legacies of Australia's biggest event

children.content: Expo '88 took over Brisbane in 1988, turning the southern banks of the Brisbane River into a multi-national bonanza. What legacy did it leave behind?

items.title: Parts of Australia on track for warmest start to winter on record

children.content: An influx of humid, tropical air has resulted in a remarkably warm and relatively wet start to winter across Australia.

image.altText: Blue skies over the ocean at Port Willunga in South Australia

items.title: Sam Short equals Grant Hackett and reminds us how far we've come

children.content: Sam Short wins the 200m, 400m, 800m and 1,500m national titles to equal the feats of Grant Hackett more than 20 years ago.

image.altText: Sam Short looks tired in the pool after a race.

items.title: Booming protein powder market opens doors for food manufacturers

children.content: A Queensland food manufacturer turns second-grade peanuts into high-protein powder as demand for protein-enhanced foods grows in Australia and overseas.

items.title: These fawning, faux-friendly celebrity interviews make my teeth ache

items.title: What Australia would have looked like when the dinosaurs roamed

items.title: SANFL players consider industrial action as hourly salary drops to $8

children.content: The SANFL Players' Association is discussing the potential of a strike in response to a 50 per cent decline in pay over the past 20 years.

items.title: Heated Rivalry, Off Campus driving ice hockey boom

children.content: Women now outnumber men as attendees to Canberra Brave ice hockey games — thanks in part to a surge in popularity for the sport inspired by the recent shows Heated Rivalry and Off Campus.
